title: Chief Technology Officer (CIM)
description:
About the Role:
As our Chief Technology Officer, you will define and lead the technological vision and strategy for our next-generation Computer-Integrated Manufacturing (CIM) platform. You will architect AI-driven solutions that power digital transformation and Industry 4.0 for global clients, with a strategic focus on the semiconductor industry. This role is critical in transitioning our product suite into intelligent, scalable, and market-leading manufacturing systems.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Establish and execute the overall technology roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals and market evolution in industrial AI and smart manufacturing.
  • Lead and scale global engineering teams across the full software development lifecycle (SDLC) — from concept and design to deployment, iteration, and support.
  • Drive architectural decisions and hands-on technical direction using modern stacks including C#, Java, Vue.js, SQL, and emerging AI/ML frameworks.
  • Foster innovation by integrating AI/ML capabilities into core CIM products to enhance predictive analytics, automation, and operational intelligence.
  • Act as the key technical voice for clients and partners, building trust through deep domain expertise and solution-oriented leadership.
  • Ensure robust technical governance, including code quality, system scalability, security, and comprehensive documentation.
  • Mentor senior technical talent and promote a culture of engineering excellence, collaboration, and continuous learning within a globally distributed team.

Essential Requirements:

  • Advanced degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field; PhD is a plus.
  • 10+ years of progressive technology leadership experience, with a proven track record in architecting and delivering enterprise-level software products, preferably in manufacturing, IoT, or industrial domains.
  • Deep, hands-on architectural experience with manufacturing execution systems (MES), equipment automation programs (EAP), or related core manufacturing platforms.
  • Deep hands-on expertise in one or more core technologies such as C#, Java, or modern front-end frameworks; practical experience with AI/ML in production environments is highly desirable.
  • Strong business acumen with experience in aligning technology development with commercial objectives and client needs in global markets.
  • Exceptional communication skills in both English and Chinese, with the ability to articulate vision to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Willingness and ability to travel internationally as required, including extended periods for key client engagements, team integration, and strategic project oversight.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Direct domain experience in semiconductor manufacturing or high-tech industrial operations.
  • Established thought leadership in Industry 4.0, digital twins, or industrial AI solutions.
  • Experience in managing geographically dispersed R&D or product engineering teams.
  • A history of successful cross-cultural collaboration and global market expansion.
